title: 10 best herbal tinctures for overeating
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for overeating are concentrated liquid extracts that contain beneficial herbs known to help manage overeating. They are typically made by soaking plant materials in alcohol or glycerin to extract the active compounds. Examples of herbs commonly used in these tinctures include St. John's Wort, Milk Thistle, and Valerian Root. St. John's Wort can help regulate appetite and reduce cravings, while Milk Thistle supports healthy liver function and metabolism. Valerian Root promotes relaxation and stress reduction, which can help address emotional eating. By incorporating herbal tinctures into a daily routine, individuals may find it easier to maintain a healthy weight, reduce the risk of obesity-related health issues, and improve overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort tincture helps with overeating because it contains active compounds that are believed to help regulate appetite and reduce cravings. Mugwort has been used traditionally to treat digestive issues, including loss of appetite and slow digestion. It is thought to stimulate the digestive system and promote the release of digestive enzymes, which can help the body break down and absorb nutrients more efficiently. This can lead to a feeling of fullness and satisfaction, reducing the urge to overeat. Additionally, mugwort may have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress-related eating and promote a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_properties: Appetite Suppression: Mugwort tincture can help reduce excessive hunger, thanks to its bitter constituents like absinthin and anabsinthin, which stimulate digestive juices and bile production, ultimately signaling satiety to the brain.

Carminative Effect: Mugwort helps alleviate bloating and gastric discomfort, as its volatile oils, such as thujone and camphor, promote gut motility and reduce inflammation, making it easier to manage overeating-induced digestive issues.

Nervine Action: Mugwort has a calming effect on the nervous system, containing compounds like flavonoids and essential oils, which can help mitigate stress-related overeating by promoting relaxation and emotional balance.

Diuretic Properties: Mugwort tincture supports kidney function and fluid balance, as it contains compounds like potassium and volatile oils, aiding in the elimination of excess fluids and waste products that may contribute to water retention and weight gain.

Blood Sugar Regulation: Mugwort tincture may help stabilize blood sugar levels, as it contains active constituents like alkaloids and phenolic acids, which can improve insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake, ultimately reducing cravings for sugary foods.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of mugwort tincture are used in making medicinal tea for overeating due to their bitter taste, which can help to suppress appetite.
Rhyzomes: Mugwort rhyzomes are used in tea for overeating because they contain active compounds that can help regulate digestion and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of mugwort tincture are used in medicinal tea for overeating because they have calming properties that can help reduce anxiety and stress-related overeating.
Seeds: Mugwort seeds are used in tea for overeating as they are rich in nutrients and can help regulate metabolism and promote weight loss.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh mugwort leaves and stems, or measure 1/2 cup of dried mugwort, and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over the mugwort, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Secure a lid on the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle or jar, discarding the spent mugwort.
Label the tincture with the ingredients, date, and recommended dosage (typically 1-2 mL up to three times daily before meals).

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tincture helps with overeating because it has been shown to reduce cravings and promote feelings of fullness. The natural compounds found in peppermint, such as menthol and limonene, are thought to help regulate appetite and decrease hunger. Additionally, peppermint has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help reduce stress-related overeating. By taking a few drops of peppermint tincture before meals, individuals may find that they are better able to control their food intake and make healthier choices. Furthermore, the refreshing and invigorating scent of peppermint can help to boost energy levels and improve mood, making it easier to resist the temptation to overeat.
remedy_properties: Appetite suppression: Peppermint tincture can help reduce overeating by promoting feelings of fullness, thanks to its active constituents like menthol and menthone.
Digestive soother: Peppermint's calming effect on the gastrointestinal tract, due to menthol and rosmarinic acid, can alleviate discomfort and help control overeating caused by indigestion.

Mood enhancer: Peppermint tincture may help improve mood and reduce stress, which can contribute to overeating; its active compounds, including menthol and flavonoids, play a role in this mood-enhancing effect.
Energy booster: Peppermint can increase energy levels and mental clarity, as menthol and other volatile oils stimulate the central nervous system, potentially reducing overeating caused by fatigue.
Nausea reduction: Peppermint tincture can alleviate nausea and vomiting, which may help control overeating; menthol and other active constituents contribute to this property.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for overeating due to their ability to aid digestion and reduce feelings of fullness.
Flowers: The flowers of the peppermint plant are used in medicinal tea for overeating as they contain menthol, which can help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems of the peppermint plant are used in medicinal tea for overeating as they contain essential oils that can help relax the gastrointestinal tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.

Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are not typically used in medicinal tea for overeating, as they contain higher levels of menthol which can be overstimulating and counterproductive for digestion.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves and stems, then place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the peppermint, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.

Transfer the resulting tincture to a dark glass bottle, label it with the ingredients and date, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 dropperfuls before meals to help control overeating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tincture helps with overeating because it promotes healthy digestion and liver function. The herb contains taraxacum, a compound that supports the production of bile, which is essential for breaking down fats in the stomach. By improving the digestion process, dandelion tincture can help regulate appetite and reduce overeating. Additionally, dandelion has diuretic properties that can help eliminate excess fluids from the body, reducing bloating and promoting a feeling of lightness. This can help individuals control their food intake and make healthier choices. Furthermore, dandelion is rich in vitamins and minerals that can help boost energy levels and overall health, making it an excellent addition to a balanced diet and lifestyle.
remedy_properties: Appetite suppression: Dandelion tincture may help reduce overeating by promoting feelings of fullness, thanks to its high fiber content.

Blood sugar regulation: Dandelion tincture can help maintain healthy blood sugar levels, which may prevent cravings and promote balanced eating habits, due to the presence of inulin and other polysaccharides.

Detoxification support: Dandelion tincture aids the liver in eliminating toxins, which can contribute to overall well-being and reduced overeating, as a result of its bitter compounds like taraxacin and taraxacerin.
Mild diuretic effect: Dandelion tincture can help reduce water retention, which may contribute to weight loss and a feeling of lightness, due to its potassium-sparing diuretic properties.

Improved digestion: Dandelion tincture can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which may help prevent overeating by enhancing nutrient absorption and promoting a sense of satisfaction after meals, resulting from its sesquiterpene lactones and taraxasterol content.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelion are used to make medicinal tea for overeating because they can help improve digestion and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are used in medicinal tea for overeating as they are rich in antioxidants and can help regulate appetite and promote weight loss.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers are used in medicinal tea for overeating because they contain bioactive compounds that can help reduce bloating and improve digestion.
Buds: The buds of dandelion are used in medicinal tea for overeating as they are rich in vitamins and minerals, and can help regulate blood sugar levels.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds are used in medicinal tea for overeating because they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and improve gut health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh dandelion leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Chop the dandelion leaves and flowers, then fill a clean glass jar 3/4 full with the diced herbs.
Pour 80-proof vodka over the diced herbs until the jar is full, leaving about 1 inch of space at the top.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

After 4-6 weeks, strain the liquid from the herbs using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, then store the resulting dandelion tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Recommended dosage is 1-2 mL, three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tincture helps with overeating because it can help regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. This is important because spikes and crashes in blood sugar can trigger cravings and overeating. By stabilizing blood sugar, cinnamon tincture can help reduce these cravings and promote a feeling of fullness. Additionally, cinnamon has been shown to have appetite-suppressing properties, making it a useful tool in managing overeating. The herbal tincture form also allows for quick absorption into the bloodstream, providing fast-acting relief. Overall, cinnamon tincture can be a helpful addition to a balanced diet and lifestyle for those looking to manage their overeating habits.
remedy_properties: Appetite suppression: Cinnamon tincture can help reduce hunger and cravings, which is important for overeating as it can help individuals eat less and consume fewer calories. The active constituents responsible for this property include cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.

Blood sugar regulation: Cinnamon tincture can help regulate blood sugar levels, which is important for overeating because it can prevent spikes and crashes in blood sugar that can lead to increased hunger and cravings. The active constituents responsible for this property include methylhydroxychalcone polymers and cinnamaldehyde.

Improved insulin sensitivity: Cinnamon tincture can help improve insulin sensitivity, which is important for overeating because it can help the body better regulate blood sugar levels and prevent insulin resistance. The active constituents responsible for this property include cinnamaldehyde and epicatechin.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Cinnamon tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which is important for overeating because chronic inflammation can contribute to obesity and related health issues. The active constituents responsible for this property include cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.

Digestive support: Cinnamon tincture can help support healthy digestion, which is important for overeating because it can prevent digestive discomfort and promote satiety. The active constituents responsible for this property include cinnamaldehyde and tannins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants, the roots of cinnamon tincture plant can help regulate blood sugar levels and reduce overeating.
Bark: Containing beneficial oils and compounds, the bark of cinnamon tincture plant aids in digestion and reduces appetite.
Seeds: High in fiber, the seeds of cinnamon tincture plant can help control hunger and promote a feeling of fullness.
Leaves: With anti-inflammatory properties, the leaves of cinnamon tincture plant can help regulate metabolism and prevent overeating.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh cinnamon bark into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum over the cinnamon, ensuring it is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and take 1-2 mL before meals to help control overeating.

remedy_properties: Appetite suppression: Caraway tincture can help reduce overeating by suppressing appetite, due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids and monoterpenes.
Digestive aid: Caraway tincture promotes healthy digestion, which can help prevent overeating caused by indigestion or bloating, thanks to its active compounds such as carvol and carvone.

Anti-inflammatory: Caraway tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate gastrointestinal inflammation, which may contribute to overeating, due to the presence of active constituents like limonene and pinene.
Antispasmodic: Caraway tincture can help relieve gastrointestinal spasms and cramps, which can lead to overeating, by containing active compounds like carvone and limonene.

Anxiolytic: Caraway tincture's anxiolytic effects can help reduce anxiety and stress-related overeating by containing active constituents like flavonoids and monoterpenes, which promote relaxation and improve mood.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tincture helps with overeating because it addresses the root causes of overeating, such as poor digestion and cravings. Caraway seeds, from which the tincture is derived, have been used for centuries to soothe the digestive system, reduce bloating, and alleviate gas. By improving digestion and nutrient absorption, caraway tincture can help regulate appetite and promote a feeling of fullness, reducing the likelihood of overeating. Additionally, caraway seeds contain compounds that may help curb cravings, making it an effective tool for managing overeating and promoting a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for overeating due to their carminative properties that help in reducing bloating and gas.
Leaves: The leaves of the caraway tincture plant are used in making medicinal tea for overeating as they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help in soothing the digestive tract.
Rhyzomes: Caraway rhyzomes are rich in antioxidants and are used in medicinal tea for overeating to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Flowers: The flowers of the caraway tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for overeating as they contain essential oils that help in stimulating digestion and reducing bloating.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried caraway seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the caraway seeds until they are completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the name, ingredients, and date, and store it in a cool, dark place. Take 1-2 mL of the tincture before meals to help with overeating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with overeating because it contains a volatile oil that can help suppress appetite and promote feelings of fullness. Fennel has long been used as a digestive aid, and its carminative properties can help reduce bloating and gas. Additionally, fennel is believed to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help the body break down food more efficiently. This can lead to a decrease in food intake and a more balanced appetite. Furthermore, fennel may help regulate blood sugar levels, which can help prevent cravings and overeating. Overall, fennel tincture can be a useful tool in managing overeating and promoting healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Appetite Suppression: Fennel tincture can help reduce hunger and promote a feeling of fullness, making it easier to control overeating. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole.

Carminative Effect: Fennel tincture can help alleviate symptoms of bloating and gas, which are often associated with overeating. The active constituents responsible for this property include fenchone, estragole, and limonene.

Digestive Aid: Fennel tincture can improve digestion and nutrient absorption, reducing the likelihood of overeating due to discomfort or indigestion. The tincture contains volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that contribute to this property.

Anti-inflammatory Effect: Fennel tincture can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with overeating. The active constituents responsible for this property include anethole, fenchone, and estragole.

Mood Enhancement: Fennel tincture can help improve mood and reduce stress, which can help prevent emotional eating. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in volatile oils, which can help suppress appetite and reduce bloating, making them ideal for managing overeating.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant contain high levels of antioxidants and vitamins, aiding in digestion and reducing inflammation that can contribute to overeating.
Stems: Fennel stems have a mild anise flavor and can help stimulate digestion, making them useful for managing overeating and promoting healthy gut function.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain various beneficial compounds that can help regulate appetite, reduce bloating, and promote overall gut health.
Rhizomes: Fennel rhizomes, or underground stems, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate digestive issues, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for overeating.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of fresh fennel leaves and roots, placing them in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the fennel, ensuring the plant material is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the finished fennel tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, using 1-2 mL doses as needed before meals to aid digestion and reduce overeating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with overeating because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is known to suppress appetite and cravings. It works by stimulating the production of hormones that signal fullness to the brain, reducing the likelihood of overeating. Additionally, licorice tincture has been shown to help regulate blood sugar levels, preventing spikes and crashes that can lead to increased food intake. By promoting a feeling of satiety and stabilizing blood sugar, licorice tincture can be a useful tool in managing overeating and promoting healthy eating habits.
remedy_properties: Appetite suppression: Licorice tincture can help reduce hunger and cravings, making it easier to stick to a healthy eating plan. This is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, a compound that can help regulate blood sugar levels and decrease appetite.

Stomach soothing: Licorice tincture can help alleviate digestive discomfort, which can be a common side effect of overeating. The active constituent responsible for this property is known as carbenoxolone, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the stomach lining.

Adrenal support: Licorice tincture can help regulate cortisol levels, which can be affected by overeating and stress. The active compound responsible for this property is glycyrrhizin, which can help support adrenal function and reduce the impact of stress on the body.

Blood sugar balance: Licorice tincture can help regulate blood sugar levels, which can be affected by overeating and unhealthy food choices. This is due to the presence of amorfrutins, which have been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce blood sugar levels.

Mood enhancement: Licorice tincture can help improve mood and reduce anxiety, which can be associated with overeating and emotional eating. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, which have been shown to have a positive effect on mood and reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tincture plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for overeating due to their ability to help control appetite and reduce sugar cravings.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tincture plant are often used in medicinal tea for overeating as they contain active compounds that can help regulate metabolism and promote weight loss.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for overeating because they contain beneficial properties that can help suppress appetite and promote digestion.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the licorice tincture plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for overeating as they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that can help reduce inflammation and regulate blood sugar levels.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried licorice root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the licorice root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the mixture gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid. Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle.
Take 1-2 mL of the tincture before meals to help control overeating, as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tincture helps with overeating because it contains powerful bioactive compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which are known to suppress appetite and reduce hunger. These compounds work by increasing the feeling of fullness and satiety, reducing the desire to eat excessively. Additionally, ginger has been shown to improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help regulate appetite and reduce overeating. Furthermore, ginger has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including nausea, bloating, and indigestion, which can contribute to overeating. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing hunger, ginger tincture can be an effective tool in managing overeating and promoting a healthy weight.
remedy_properties: Appetite suppression: Ginger tincture can help control overeating by reducing appetite and promoting feelings of fullness, due to the presence of active constituents like gingerols and shogaols.
Nausea reduction: Ginger tincture is known to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting, which can be beneficial for overeating, as it contains bioactive compounds such as 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol.

Blood sugar regulation: Ginger tincture can help maintain healthy blood sugar levels, which may prevent overeating caused by rapid spikes and crashes in blood sugar, thanks to active components like gingerols and paradols.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger tincture has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, promoting healthy digestion and reducing discomfort after overeating, due to the presence of gingerols and shogaols.

Gastrointestinal motility enhancement: Ginger tincture can help improve gastrointestinal motility, aiding in digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help prevent overeating by promoting a feeling of satisfaction after meals, thanks to the active constituents gingerols and shogaols.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal tea for overeating due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to aid digestion.
Leaves: Ginger leaves have been used in teas to help regulate appetite and alleviate digestive discomfort associated with overeating.
Flowers: Ginger flowers can be used to make a soothing tea that may help reduce bloating and improve digestion after overeating.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are sometimes included in medicinal teas for overeating, as they can help stimulate digestion and reduce feelings of fullness.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh ginger root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Add 2 cups of 80 proof vodka or rum to the jar, ensuring the ginger is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid and discarding the ginger solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL before meals to aid in digestion and reduce overeating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tincture helps with overeating because it contains natural compounds that can help regulate appetite and reduce cravings. Spearmint has been shown to help balance hormones that can contribute to overeating, particularly in women with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Additionally, spearmint has calming and soothing properties that can help reduce stress-related eating. The use of spearmint tincture as a natural remedy for overeating can be a helpful addition to a balanced diet and exercise routine, promoting a healthier relationship with food and supporting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Appetite suppression: Spearmint tincture can help reduce overeating by suppressing appetite, thanks to the active constituent carvone, which is known to influence satiety hormones.

Digestive aid: Spearmint tincture promotes healthy digestion, helping the body better process food and reducing the likelihood of overeating due to indigestion or discomfort, with menthol and flavonoids contributing to its digestive benefits.

Stress reduction: Spearmint tincture can alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often linked to overeating, by increasing feelings of calm and relaxation, as rosmarinic acid and other antioxidants in spearmint have been shown to have stress-reducing properties.

Blood sugar regulation: Spearmint tincture helps maintain healthy blood sugar levels, preventing spikes and crashes that may lead to overeating, by promoting insulin sensitivity, with rosmarinic acid and other polyphenols contributing to this effect.

Anti-inflammatory: Spearmint tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating symptoms that may contribute to overeating, with rosmarinic acid and other antioxidants responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the spearmint plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for overeating due to their carminative properties, which help to relieve digestive discomfort and bloating.
Flowers: Spearmint flowers contain volatile oils that have antispasmodic effects, making them useful for soothing an upset stomach and reducing overeating.
Seeds: Spearmint seeds are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to help regulate appetite and reduce overeating.
Stems: The stems of the spearmint plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of overeating.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of fresh spearmint leaves and stems, and place them in a clean jar.
Pour 1 cup of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) before meals to help control overeating.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the plant material and keeping the liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle, using 1-2 mL doses up to three times daily before meals to help control overeating.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat overeating with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of herbs known to support healthy digestion and satiety. Look for a tincture containing herbs such as milk thistle, dandelion, and gentian. Milk thistle aids in liver function, promoting the metabolism of fats and sugars. Dandelion supports healthy digestion and elimination, while gentian stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, reducing the likelihood of overeating. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overeating that are treated with herbal tinctures include obesity, indigestion, and irritable bowel syndrome. Tinctures made from herbs such as milk thistle, dandelion, and burdock root can help support liver function and promote healthy digestion. Peppermint and ginger tinctures can alleviate symptoms of indigestion, while licorice root and chamomile tinctures can help soothe an upset stomach. Additionally, tinctures made from herbs like turmeric and cinnamon can help regulate blood sugar levels, which can be beneficial for those who overeat due to stress or emotional eating.
